annotations.label: Algebraic number theory

anchor.snippet: Algebraic number theory is a branch of number theory that uses the techniques of abstract algebra

annotations.note: A description of the discipline is not a formalizable statement, though its central object `NumberField` is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Typical Diophantine problem

anchor.snippet: A typical Diophantine problem is to find two integers

annotations.note: An informal motivating example, not a specific theorem present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pythagorean triples solve x²+y²=z²

anchor.snippet: the solutions to the quadratic Diophantine equation

annotations.note: `PythagoreanTriple` is defined and fully classified via `PythagoreanTriple.classification`.

annotations.label: Linear Diophantine equations via Euclidean algorithm

anchor.snippet: Solutions to linear Diophantine equations

annotations.note: Bézout's identity `Int.gcd_eq_gcd_ab` (with computable `gcdA`/`gcdB`) provides the solutions, but there is no packaged general linear-Diophantine solver.

annotations.label: Fermat's Last Theorem

anchor.snippet: Fermat's Last Theorem was first conjectured by Pierre de Fermat

annotations.note: The `FermatLastTheorem` statement is defined and proven only for small exponents (n=3 via `fermatLastTheoremThree`, n=4 in FLT.Four); the full theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: First class number formula for quadratic forms

anchor.snippet: proved the first class number formula

annotations.note: Mathlib has the general analytic class number formula for number fields, of which Dirichlet's quadratic-forms version is a special case, but not that historical form itself.

annotations.label: Dirichlet unit theorem

anchor.snippet: he proved the Dirichlet unit theorem

annotations.note: The Dirichlet unit theorem is formalized as `NumberField.Units.exist_unique_eq_mul_prod` with the unit `rank` and `fundSystem`.

annotations.label: Dirichlet's approximation theorem

anchor.snippet: Dirichlet's approximation theorem

annotations.note: Dirichlet's approximation theorem is `Real.exists_int_int_abs_mul_sub_le` (and `AddCircle.exists_norm_nsmul_le`).

annotations.label: Dedekind's definition of an ideal

anchor.snippet: Dedekind defined an ideal as a subset of a set of numbers

annotations.note: `Ideal R` (a submodule closed under multiplication by ring elements) is the standard Mathlib definition.

annotations.label: Artin reciprocity law

anchor.snippet: Emil Artin established the Artin reciprocity law

annotations.note: No Artin reciprocity law / class field theory main theorem is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Modularity theorem

anchor.snippet: The resulting modularity theorem

annotations.note: The modularity theorem is not in Mathlib (an external FLT project is pursuing a special case).

annotations.label: Wiles' proof of modularity for semistable curves

anchor.snippet: Andrew Wiles provided a proof of the modularity theorem for semistable elliptic curves

annotations.note: Modularity of semistable elliptic curves is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Prime element

anchor.snippet: a prime element is an element of the ring satisfying the key property of prime numbers

annotations.note: `Prime p` (p divides a product implies it divides a factor) is defined for commutative monoids with zero.

annotations.label: Associate elements

anchor.snippet: Numbers such as p and up are said to be associate

annotations.note: `Associated x y` (differ by a unit) is defined exactly as in the article.

annotations.label: Unique factorization domain

anchor.snippet: one adopts the definition of unique factorization used in unique factorization domains

annotations.note: Mathlib uses `UniqueFactorizationMonoid`, which generalizes the UFD notion to commutative monoids with zero.

annotations.label: Trivial ideal class group implies UFD

anchor.snippet: When the ideal class group is trivial, the ring is a UFD

annotations.note: `card_classGroup_eq_one_iff` proves the stronger equivalence (trivial class group ⟺ PID) for Dedekind domains, and PID ⟹ UFD holds in Mathlib, but the exact UFD phrasing is not a single lemma.

annotations.label: Irreducible element

anchor.snippet: An irreducible element x is an element such that if x = yz

annotations.note: `Irreducible p` is defined as a non-unit whose every factorization has a unit factor.

annotations.label: Failure of unique factorization in Z[√-5]

anchor.snippet: consider the ring Z [√ -5 ]

annotations.note: The ring `Zsqrtd` (ℤ[√d]) is defined, but the specific non-unique-factorization example 6 = 2·3 = (1+√-5)(1-√-5) is not proven in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Unique factorization into prime ideals (Dedekind domains)

anchor.snippet: If I is an ideal in O , then there is always a factorization

annotations.note: Ideals of a Dedekind domain form a `UniqueFactorizationMonoid` via `Ideal.uniqueFactorizationMonoid`, giving unique factorization into prime ideals.

annotations.label: Non-principal prime ideal (2, 1+√-5)

anchor.snippet: is a prime ideal which cannot be generated by a single element

annotations.note: This specific non-principal prime ideal in ℤ[√-5] is not constructed in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ideal number

anchor.snippet: A generator of this principal ideal is called an ideal number

annotations.note: Kummer's historical 'ideal number' notion is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Primes in Gaussian integers via sums of two squares

anchor.snippet: is provided by Fermat's theorem on sums of two squares

annotations.note: Fermat's two-squares theorem is `Nat.Prime.sq_add_sq`, with the Gaussian-prime characterization `GaussianInt.prime_iff_mod_four_eq_three_of_nat_prime`.

annotations.label: Unique factorization fails iff non-principal prime ideals exist

anchor.snippet: Unique factorization fails in O if and only if there are prime ideals that fail to be principal

annotations.note: `card_classGroup_eq_one_iff` gives unique factorization (class number 1) ⟺ every ideal is principal for Dedekind domains, but the exact 'non-principal prime ideal' phrasing is not a separate lemma.

annotations.label: Fractional ideal

anchor.snippet: A fractional ideal is an additive subgroup J of K which is closed under multiplication

annotations.note: `FractionalIdeal` is defined as a submodule of the fraction field with a common denominator.

annotations.label: Ideal class group

anchor.snippet: by this subgroup is the ideal class group

annotations.note: `ClassGroup R` is defined as invertible fractional ideals modulo principal ideals.

annotations.label: Class number

anchor.snippet: The number of elements in the class group is called the class number

annotations.note: `NumberField.classNumber` is defined as the cardinality of the class group of `𝓞 K`.

annotations.label: Class number of Q(√-5) is 2

anchor.snippet: The class number of Q (√ -5 ) is 2

annotations.note: This specific class-number computation for ℚ(√-5) is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Divisor group

anchor.snippet: The divisor group Div K is defined to be the free abelian group generated by the prime ideals

annotations.note: No explicit free-abelian-group divisor group Div_K on prime ideals is defined (only `HeightOneSpectrum` and ideal factorization).

annotations.label: Kernel and cokernel of div

anchor.snippet: The kernel of div is the group of units in O

annotations.note: The class group is defined precisely as such a cokernel (of `toPrincipalIdeal`), but the explicit divisor-map exact sequence with kernel = units is not stated as one result.

annotations.label: Real and complex embeddings

anchor.snippet: These are called real embeddings and complex embeddings

annotations.note: Real vs complex embeddings/places are captured by `NumberField.ComplexEmbedding.IsReal` and `InfinitePlace.IsReal`/`IsComplex`.

annotations.label: Real quadratic field

anchor.snippet: A real quadratic field Q (√ a )

annotations.note: Mathlib has `IsTotallyReal` and `Zsqrtd`, but no dedicated 'real quadratic field' definition.

annotations.label: Imaginary quadratic field

anchor.snippet: an imaginary quadratic field Q (√ − a ) admits no real embeddings

annotations.note: `IsTotallyComplex` (no real embeddings) exists, but there is no dedicated 'imaginary quadratic field' definition.

annotations.label: Signature of a number field

anchor.snippet: The signature of K is the pair

annotations.note: Both components `nrRealPlaces` and `nrComplexPlaces` are defined, but the pair (r₁,r₂) is not bundled as a 'signature'.

annotations.label: r₁ + 2r₂ = d

anchor.snippet: It is a theorem that

annotations.note: `card_add_two_mul_card_eq_rank`: nrRealPlaces K + 2·nrComplexPlaces K = finrank ℚ K.

annotations.label: Minkowski embedding

anchor.snippet: This is called the Minkowski embedding

annotations.note: The Minkowski embedding is `NumberField.mixedEmbedding` (into ℝ^r₁ × ℂ^r₂), with `canonicalEmbedding` the variant into ℂ^d.

annotations.label: Minkowski space

anchor.snippet: is a real vector space of dimension d called Minkowski space

annotations.note: `mixedSpace K = (ℝ^r₁) × (ℂ^r₂)` is the codomain of the mixed/Minkowski embedding.

annotations.label: Discriminant

anchor.snippet: is the discriminant of O

annotations.note: `NumberField.discr` is the discriminant of the ring of integers.

annotations.label: p-adic absolute value

anchor.snippet: there are p-adic absolute value functions

annotations.note: `padicNorm p q` is the p-adic absolute value on ℚ (with `padicNormE` on ℚ_p).

annotations.label: Ostrowski's theorem

anchor.snippet: Ostrowski's theorem states that these are all possible absolute value functions

annotations.note: Ostrowski's theorem is `Rat.AbsoluteValue.equiv_real_or_padic`.

annotations.label: Place of a number field

anchor.snippet: A place of an algebraic number field is an equivalence class of absolute value functions

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `InfinitePlace` and `FinitePlace` separately but has no single unified 'place' type as an equivalence class of absolute values.

annotations.label: Finite and infinite places

anchor.snippet: These are called finite places

annotations.note: `NumberField.FinitePlace` and `NumberField.InfinitePlace` are both defined.

annotations.label: Adele ring

anchor.snippet: Considering all the places of the field together produces the adele ring

annotations.note: `NumberField.AdeleRing` is the product of the infinite and finite adele rings.

annotations.label: Geometric analogy for places at infinity

anchor.snippet: There is a geometric analogy for places at infinity

annotations.note: An informal geometric analogy, not a formal statement.

annotations.label: Function field of the projective line

anchor.snippet: then its function field is isomorphic to

annotations.note: `FunctionField` and `RatFunc` (= k(t)) are defined, but the geometric isomorphism with the function field of ℙ¹ is not stated.

annotations.label: Units in rings of integers

anchor.snippet: The integers have only two units

annotations.note: `Int.isUnit_iff`: a unit of ℤ is exactly 1 or -1.

annotations.label: Unit group is finitely generated abelian

anchor.snippet: the group of units of O , denoted O × , is a finitely generated abelian group

annotations.note: The Dirichlet unit theorem gives `(𝓞 K)ˣ ≅ torsion × ℤ^rank`, hence finitely generated abelian.

annotations.label: Regulator

anchor.snippet: The covolume of this lattice is the regulator of the number field

annotations.note: `NumberField.Units.regulator` is defined as the covolume of the unit lattice.

annotations.label: Dedekind zeta function

anchor.snippet: The Dedekind zeta function of a number field

annotations.note: `NumberField.dedekindZeta` is defined.

annotations.label: Completion at a place gives a complete field

anchor.snippet: Completing a number field K at a place w gives a complete field

annotations.note: `adicCompletion K v` is the completion at a finite place (a complete valued field via `UniformSpace.Completion`); infinite places use `NumberField.Completion`.

annotations.label: Kronecker–Weber theorem

anchor.snippet: the Kronecker–Weber theorem can be deduced easily

annotations.note: The Kronecker–Weber theorem is not formalized in Mathlib (only listed as a target).

annotations.label: Finiteness of the class group

anchor.snippet: the ideal class group of an algebraic number field K is finite

annotations.note: The class group of a number field's ring of integers is given a `Fintype` instance, proving finiteness.

annotations.label: Class number (order of class group)

anchor.snippet: The order of the class group is called the class number

annotations.note: `NumberField.classNumber` is the order (cardinality) of the class group.

annotations.label: Dirichlet's unit theorem

anchor.snippet: Dirichlet's unit theorem provides a description of the structure of the multiplicative group of units

annotations.note: Dirichlet's unit theorem describing the structure of `(𝓞 K)ˣ` is `exist_unique_eq_mul_prod`.

annotations.label: Law of quadratic reciprocity

anchor.snippet: the law of quadratic reciprocity for positive odd primes states

annotations.note: `legendreSym.quadratic_reciprocity`: (q|p)(p|q) = (-1)^((p/2)(q/2)) for distinct odd primes.

annotations.label: Reciprocity law

anchor.snippet: A reciprocity law is a generalization of the law of quadratic reciprocity

annotations.note: Only quadratic (and Jacobi-symbol) reciprocity is formalized; the general reciprocity-law concept (Artin/higher) is not.

annotations.label: Class number formula

anchor.snippet: The class number formula relates many important invariants of a number field

annotations.note: The analytic class number formula (residue of the Dedekind zeta at s=1) is `tendsto_sub_one_mul_dedekindZeta_nhdsGT`.
